items.headline: City Council reviews youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: The Council reviewed status reports on San José's Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including youth empowerment programs and a Safe Summer Initiative Grant, plus an update on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. These reports outline how the city is investing in services for young people and improving library access.

items.headline: Project at 333 Franklin Street delayed, hearing rescheduled
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a development project at 333 Franklin Street was postponed from April 8 and will be rescheduled for a later date. The project will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is set.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what this construction project entails and where it will take place in Los Gatos.

items.headline: City approves grant for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts
items.summary: The Council approved a California Natural Resources Agency Grant for the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming at a community institution in San José.

items.headline: City moves forward with Bascom Avenue traffic signal safety project
items.summary: The Council extended a consultant agreement with DKS Associates for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, a safety improvement addressing the intersection of vehicle and train traffic. This project aims to reduce accidents at a critical crossing.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council reviewed Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, which guides biking and pedestrian infrastructure improvements across Cupertino. The revised plan includes new scoring criteria that will determine which projects get built first, affecting how residents can walk and bike around town.

items.headline: Council Changes Vacancy Ordinance Rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled, though specific details on what changes were proposed aren't fully clear from the agenda. This could affect how future council seats are appointed if a member leaves office.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartments approved for East St John Street
items.summary: Trillium is building a nearly $9M senior housing complex with 65 units at 678 E St John St, addressing a critical housing need for older adults in San José. This is the largest single housing project in this permit batch.
